danger_signals.meaning: Active threat to player; combat encounter imminent
Narrative antagonist threat; dynamic danger marker

danger_signals.visual_form: Darkened environments, dungeon aesthetic across 40+ floors
danger_signals.meaning: World corrupted by Essence of Power; pervasive danger state

danger_signals.visual_form: Distinct area types: Ruins, Tower, Cave, Dark Zone, Fortress
danger_signals.meaning: Escalating difficulty and danger progression through descent

safety_signals.meaning: Navigation aid; player control and orientation in labyrinth

safety_signals.visual_form: Equipment and consumable pickups within dungeons
safety_signals.meaning: Player progression and combat capability increase
Defined success state; narrative purpose provides direction

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: The Tower (central landmark); Dragon guardian imagery
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ruins zone; underground submerged kingdom aesthetic; Tower structure piercing through dungeon areas
Player sprite; primary avatar throughout dungeon crawl

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Magician identity; sorceress archetype; reveals as vengeful antagonist
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Character sprite appears in pursuit encounters; alternate playable character in Dark Revenant remake
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Repeated encounters throughout maze; narrative presence as pursuing threat

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Corrupted ruler; Essence of Power possession
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Transformed into hideous monster after grasping Essence
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Tower origin; implicit presence in dungeon corruption state

Tome discovery; research activity; initial Tower investigation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: King organizes secret army; scholars decipher ancient language

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Combat destruction; Dragon sacrifice; Essence of Power unleashed
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Dragon destroys Essence to prevent corruption; catastrophic transformation begins

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sunken kingdom underground; corrupted ruins; multiple dungeon zones (Ruins, Tower, Cave, Dark Zone, Fortress); darkness and decay
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: World forgotten on surface; inhabitants disappeared; cursed labyrinth persists

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Active game state (Ares and Dela falling into Ruins)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dungeon maze with 40+ floors; monster encounters; trap hazards; top-down overhead perspective
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ares escapes; Dela pursues in revenge; both seek maze exit; multiple encounters and dialogue events

environmental_storytelling.example: Five distinct area types (Ruins, Tower, Cave, Dark Zone, Fortress) communicate dungeon narrative progression and deepening danger as player descends 40+ floors
Ancient kingdom backstory (Vittoria, Tower, Dragon, Bistalle's ambition) establishes why cursed labyrinth exists; Essence of Power corruption explains monster presence and world corruption
Dela Delon repeatedly encounters Ares throughout descent; visual meetings reinforce antagonism and revenge narrative; dialogue/event variations show relationship complexity
Sunken underground kingdom, darkened environments, and maze complexity visually communicate that world has been corrupted and transformed by Essence of Power
Labyrinth structure with clear escape goal provides implicit storytelling; player must exit; 40+ floors = significant challenge ahead

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Must escape labyrinth; moves forward/backward, turns, strafes, attacks via mouse control system; seeks exit path
Actively pursues Ares throughout descent; magic attacks cause ground collapse; meets Ares repeatedly at decision points; can be helped or left to danger (ending variance)
Various types distributed across 40+ floors; engage in real-time combat; present obstacles to escape
Sacrificed itself to destroy Essence; prevented full kingdom corruption; implicit presence in dungeon design
Transformed into hideous monster upon grasping Essence; implicit presence as ultimate source of dungeon curse

notes: Brandish is a real-time overhead dungeon crawler where world-state is communicated primarily through environmental design (five distinct zones, 40+ floors) and narrative context. The game uses visual progression through descending floors and zone transitions to communicate danger escalation. Faction identity is established through character archetypes (warrior Ares vs. sorceress Dela) and historical backstory (Kingdom of Vittoria, Dragon guardian, corrupted King Bistalle). The top-down perspective with automap provides navigation clarity despite maze complexity. Nintendo of America censorship affected visual presentation of Dela's costume and plot elements in SNES English version. Character encounter mechanics and ending variance (helping/abandoning Dela) create behavioral world-state changes. Curse/corruption is communicated through sunken underground aesthetic and monster presence rather than explicit visual corruption effects.
